diary:
Woke to brilliant sunshine and went through my usual Green Lake routine—coffee tasted better today, maybe because of the weather. The commute to SoDo was smooth; I caught the tail end of the morning rush. My shift was steady, nothing dramatic. A few passengers mentioned the World Cup match starting at eleven, and I could hear the bars downtown gearing up even from the road. The Light Rail closure didn't affect my routes too much, though I noticed more foot traffic than usual.

After work I grabbed a quick snack and headed straight to the trail. The lake was gorgeous this evening—that perfect early-June light where everything looks painterly. My run felt good, legs strong. Saw a pair of great blue herons near the water's edge, which I jotted down in my notes later. Dinner was simple, just pasta and greens. Spent the evening with my bird journal and a book, feeling pleasantly tired. There's something grounding about days like this—ordinary, but full.
thought_process: The weather lifted my mood from the start; I could feel the difference in my own pace. I considered heading downtown to catch some of the World Cup buzz at a bar, but honestly, after eight hours of driving and managing passengers, I needed the quiet of my own space and the trail. The Light Rail maintenance didn't really impact me since I drive a bus anyway, but I was aware of it—part of the mental map I keep of the city. My evening priorities shifted toward physical movement and solitude rather than socializing, which felt right. The run cleared my head in that way only exercise does, and birdwatching grounds me when I'm feeling scattered. It was a day that asked nothing urgent of me, and I was grateful for that simplicity.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
